acceptance_targets: [ch03_f001] Pass if the revised lines at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:97 let a cold reader map each named medication or medical expense to the correct canon patient. If Sinemet/carbidopa-levodopa remains in the paragraph, it must be explicitly tied to Lola or another Parkinson's patient; if the sentence remains about Burt, every listed expense must be legibly plausible for Burt's hearing/chronic-pain/dementia profile.

[ch03_f003] Pass if the ending after snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:267 adds at least one materially new pressure element that is not already explicit in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:221-229, and does not recap Carmen's evidence trail plus the full option list in summary form. Fail if a reader reaches the last paragraph having learned only a cleaner restatement of the meeting discussion.

[PROSE_AUTOCOMM_CH03] PASS if: (a) none of the six cited constructions remain verbatim at lines 481, 505, 513, 531, 581, 651; (b) no more than 2 total instances of any single pattern from this set — 'not X but Y' antithetical correction, 'the specific/particular' qualifier, 'the way [pronoun] [verb]' explanatory simile, 'that was the thing' narrator pivot — remain in the chapter; (c) the narrative texture feels noticeably different from Carmen-POV chapters in its sentence architecture. FAIL if the constructions persist, are replaced with equivalent glossing formulas, or the chapter still reads as the same narrator voice as chapters 1-2.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised lines at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:97 let a cold reader map each named medication or medical expense to the correct canon patient. If Sinemet/carbidopa-levodopa remains in the paragraph, it must be explicitly tied to Lola or another Parkinson's patient; if the sentence remains about Burt, every listed expense must be legibly plausible for Burt's hearing/chronic-pain/dementia profile.
findings.problem: The money-trail paragraph assigns Burt brand-name Sinemet/carbidopa-levodopa costs even though the continuity sheet establishes Parkinson's as Lola's condition, not Burt's. In a chapter built on precise operational detail, that medical mismatch reads as a canon contradiction, not a throwaway slip.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:97 so Burt's share funds expenses consistent with Burt's established profile (hearing, chronic pain, dementia-related care), or explicitly move the Sinemet/carbidopa-levodopa detail onto Lola's share if that is the intended medication trail. Preserve the paragraph's function of showing where the ring's money goes.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the ending after snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:267 adds at least one materially new pressure element that is not already explicit in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:221-229, and does not recap Carmen's evidence trail plus the full option list in summary form. Fail if a reader reaches the last paragraph having learned only a cleaner restatement of the meeting discussion.
findings.problem: The chapter lands Carmen as a threat during the meeting, then spends the bedside ending re-summarizing the same evidence trail and option menu. Instead of buying a new turn, the close re-proves what the reader already understands.
findings.rewrite_direction: Tighten or repurpose snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:275-281, and adjust snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:267-273 if needed, so Harry's final beat does new dramatic work: a concrete first move, a sharper cost, or a destabilizing thought he did not already share at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:221-229. Do not simply re-list the count, the hall walk, the TV-room sighting, and the co-opt/confuse/discredit/wait framework.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if: (a) none of the six cited constructions remain verbatim at lines 481, 505, 513, 531, 581, 651; (b) no more than 2 total instances of any single pattern from this set — 'not X but Y' antithetical correction, 'the specific/particular' qualifier, 'the way [pronoun] [verb]' explanatory simile, 'that was the thing' narrator pivot — remain in the chapter; (c) the narrative texture feels noticeably different from Carmen-POV chapters in its sentence architecture. FAIL if the constructions persist, are replaced with equivalent glossing formulas, or the chapter still reads as the same narrator voice as chapters 1-2.
findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:481, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:505, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:513, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:531, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:581, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:651

evidence.excerpt: 481: "I said I will." She set the tablet down with the precise, slightly pointed placement that was Agnes's version of a door closing.

505: "Three times." Agnes set down a bag of alprazolam and looked at Burt directly, her glasses low on her nose, her expression not unkind but not accommodating either. "The details shift. The first time, the joints cost the man ten dollars each. The second time, twenty. Tonight, fifteen."

513: This was the thing about Burt that Harry couldn't account for in any ledger: the moments when the real Burt surfaced — funny, specific, sure of something that had happened sixty years ago — and then slid back under, and you couldn't tell whether he'd been lucid for thirty seconds or had never left whatever place he lived in now and had just produced a good performance by accident. The others were performing impairment they didn't have. Burt was the opposite. He was performing competence he was losing.

531: He looked down. She was right. He'd forgotten the compression socks again, and his ankles were swelling — not badly, not dangerously, but visibly, the skin pressing against the elastic band of his slipper socks with the kind of mild edema that Dr. Prasad would have noted on a chart and that Harry would have continued to ignore.

581: "Does she think we are sundowning?" Agnes asked. Her voice had tightened — not louder but faster, the words compressing the way they did when she was running variables, checking for the dangerous combination.

651: Harry stopped at his door — 114A, the single room, a privilege earned by seniority and the administrative assumption that he needed quiet for his condition. He wheeled in. He transferred to the bed, the movement efficient even though no one was watching and the performance was for empty walls. He pulled the thin blanket to his chest. The window unit rattled. The room was warm and close, the air thick with late August and the particular staleness of a space one person lived in and never left.

findings.problem: Full-book pattern (appears in all 18 chapters): the narration systematically explains, qualifies, and corrects its own images through recurring constructions that create a single authorial voice across all POVs. This chapter (Harry POV) should feel like a ledger — precise, clipped, controlled — but the narration uses the same explanatory apparatus as Carmen's chapters. Specific instances in this chapter: 'the precise, slightly pointed placement that was Agnes's version of a door closing' (line 481 — 'X that was Y's version of Z' formula + 'door closing' metaphor used in 3 other chapters); 'her expression not unkind but not accommodating either' (line 505 — double 'not X but Y'); 'This was the thing about Burt that Harry couldn't account for in any ledger' (line 513 — 'this was the thing' narrator pivot, appears 8 times across 6 chapters); 'not badly, not dangerously, but visibly' (line 531 — triple-qualifier 'not X, not Y, but Z'); 'not louder but faster' (line 581); 'the particular staleness of a space one person lived in' (line 651 — 'the particular X' qualifier). Six instances in one chapter creates a drone over prose that should feel like Harry's accounting mind, not a narrator explaining Harry to the reader.
findings.rewrite_direction: REPLACE — this chapter's instances should use varied alternatives. The full-book pattern should be kept to at most 1-2 instances across the entire novel (strongest candidates for retention are in chapters 1 and 15, not here). Target lines: (1) Line 481: cut 'that was Agnes's version of a door closing' — let the gesture land without the analogy. Agnes sets the tablet down with pointed precision. Period. (2) Line 505: choose one descriptor. 'Not unkind but not accommodating' is a double negative that cancels into a mild positive — just describe what her expression IS. (3) Line 513: cut 'This was the thing about Burt that Harry couldn't account for in any ledger' — begin directly with the observation about Burt's lucidity surfacing and submerging. The image is strong enough without the narrator announcing its significance. (4) Line 531: 'not badly, not dangerously, but visibly' — replace triple qualifier with one concrete descriptor of the swelling. (5) Line 581: rewrite without 'not X but Y.' (6) Line 651: cut 'the particular' — 'staleness' is already specific. Throughout, trust the images. Harry's POV should carry his precision through what he NOTICES (numbers, angles, leverage), not through the narrator explaining what he notices.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This is a dark comedy about elderly agency, institutional failure, and moral compromise in a setting most fiction treats as a punchline or a tragedy but never both. The risks the novel must take: render the comedy of octogenarians as pharmaceutical distributors without condescending to them or turning them into lovable rogues; make Carmen's escalating complicity fully legible without moralizing about it; show the bodily realities of aging — incontinence, tremor, cognitive decline, falls, the smell of a body that doesn't leave a building — with unsentimental specificity; render the economic desperation of both healthcare workers and uninsured buyers without turning either into social-issue abstractions; give the moral ambiguity of the ring genuine weight by refusing to resolve it into either 'they were wrong' or 'they were heroes.' The novel must trust readers to sit with complexity. When in doubt between too raw and too safe, choose raw. A nursing home that smells like potpourri is a nursing home written by someone who's never been in one.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism for bodily functions, aging, economic shame, institutional dysfunction, or the realities of nursing home care is a craft failure. The novel's comedy and humanity depend on rendering what polite fiction glosses over: what it smells like to clean someone who can't clean themselves, what it costs to count coins for a bus fare, what oxycodone sells for per milligram on the secondary market, what a concussion looks like on an 88-year-old skull, what it sounds like when someone with Parkinson's tries to hold a coffee cup steady, what Burt's dementia actually looks like up close. If the prose flinches from these specifics, the novel loses its authority.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the default for all dialogue. Uncontracted forms appear only for emphasis, character-specific formality (Agnes's low contraction level), Carmen under extreme stress reverting to formal speech, or deliberate register choices. In a contemporary New Jersey nursing home, stiff diction is the anomaly, not the norm.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate high-intensity scenes with reflective interiority. Vary scene count: single-scene chapters for maximum compression, three-scene chapters for range. Comic chapters (3, 9) should feel structurally different from quiet character studies (7, 8, 12, 15), which should feel different from crisis chapters (11, 16, 17). Follow a confrontation with a reflection, an escalation with a domestic scene, a comic set piece with an elegiac one. If two consecutive chapters share the same rhythm, one needs redesigning.
prose_style_profile.diction: Grounded and specific. Drug names (oxycodone, alprazolam, hydroxychloroquine), nursing procedures (med pass, pill count, charting, fall assessment), body realities (incontinence, tremor, laceration, scalp bleed). No purple prose, no literary performance. The style should feel like someone who works with their hands is telling you the truth about what she saw.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, varying by POV character. Carmen chapters are warm and sensory, anchored in the body and the immediate environment. Harry chapters are precise and calculating, with attention to numbers and angles. Agnes chapters are clinical and compressed. Lola chapters are winding and emotionally layered, with embedded memories. Earl chapters are spare and weighted, with silence doing as much work as language. Marcus chapters are kinetic, with physical movement and code-switching. Tammy chapters are flat and observational, her trained eye cataloguing what doesn't add up.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied by chapter and POV. Carmen chapters favor medium sentences with occasional long runs when she's anxious. Harry chapters are short and clipped. Comic set pieces (chapters 3 and 9) move between fast inventory and slow domestic beats. Quiet chapters (7, 12, 15) slow down and let space onto the page. No chapter should feel rhythmically identical to the one before it.
